title: D1 — Post room: voice-over chain with house clock
brief: Wire a post room in three parallel sub-chains, then set two device-state gates. CAPTURE: U 87 into the ISA One preamp (a condenser — it needs +48 V). WORKSTATION: Pro Tools on DigiLink into the HD I/O, slaved to the OCX HD house clock. MONITORING: HD I/O analog out through the Mogami loom to the m905 controller and the Genelecs. Then arm it: switch on the ISA One's +48 V (R4) — the interface locks to the master clock the moment its word-clock input is fed (R8).
objective: Record-ready VO chain: mic powered, workstation clocked, monitors fed, one clock master, everything locked.
successMessage: Locked to house clock, +48 V up, VO chain armed. (Full version will route the record path through the patchbay — bay-1 is on the desk waiting.)
notes: Eight-cable, two-gate build. R4 (phantom): the U 87 (requiresPhantom) reaches the ISA One mic input, but the ISA's +48 V control is OFF by default — wiring it silent triggers R4 until the player switches +48 V on (ADR-0001, device state). R8 (clock): the HD I/O carries digital audio on DigiLink and has an isClockSlave word-clock input; leaving that input open triggers R8 until the OCX HD master is wired to it — the last required cable. KNOWN DEPTH GAP (docs/REVIEW-ME.md §6): the record path (preamp OUT → interface analog IN) and the patchbay bay-1 are NOT wired in this level, because with the simplified catalog ports there is no valid connector route (the ISA is XLR-out, the HD I/O analog-in is DB25, and no XLR→DB25 fan-in loom or patchbay normalling is modeled). Closing that gap needs either a catalog device (an XLR→DB25 loom) or ADR-0002 patchbay normalling — an engine/content change out of scope for a content-only run. bay-1 stays on the desk as the visible promise.
